task:
[Instruction] You are given an excerpt from a script: 
 (opening:     The camera slowly zooms through a variety of insects and rocks. We follow a small yellow bug climb up a blade of grass.  As it spreads its wings to fly  we are whisked along the Australian outback and prairie by Ayers rock and eventually slow down as we approach Cody's house.)

(scene:  inside Cody's room.  The camera pans around to show Cody sleeping in his hammock.  The sound of Faloo's call is heard.  Cody hears it  jumps out of bed  and runs to the window.  He puts on his shirt and grabs his knife.)

(scene:  Cody sneaks past his mother who is in the kitchen listening to the radio.)

Announcer:    ... thundershowers are expected in the Crocodile Falls area and some of the surrounding gullies so take out your...

(scene:  Outside Cody's house.  Cody leaves the house  and closes the door behind him  but not quietly.)

Mom:     (from inside upon hearing the door) Cody!

Cody:    (whincing) Yeah mom?

Mom:     What about your breakfast?

Cody:    I've got some sandwiches in my pack.

Mom:     Well be home for supper.

Cody:    (hopping the gate) No worries mom.

(scene:  Cody runs toward the forest; Faloo's call is heard in the background.  He runs past some rock formations and enters the woods.  Birds follow him; and squak at him.)

Cody:    (to the birds) I know  I'm coming.
 (Cody jumps over a hollow log) Hustle up Nelson  Faloo's sounding the call!
 (Cody slides through a log  picks up a stick  and beats on the roof of the wombats home.) C'mon little wombats  hurry!
 (Cody continues to run through the forest with all of the animals following him.)
 (Cody arrives at the tree where Faloo has been sounding the call.)
 (to Faloo) Who's caught this time?

Faloo:   You don't know her  Cody  her name is Marahute  the great golden eagle.

Cody:    Where is she?

Faloo:   She's caught  high on a cliff in a poacher's trap.  You're the only one who can reach her.

Cody:    I'll get her loose.

Faloo:   Right-oh  hop on  no time to lose.
 (Cody hops onto Faloo and they travel through the forest and along a stream/river; more scenes of animals and the forest.)
 (They arrive at the cliff.)
 (pointing up towards the cliff) She's up on top of that ridge.  Be careful lit'l friend.

(scene:  various "time lapse" views of Cody climbing up the cliff.)
 (Cody reaches the top and sees the eagle.)

Cody:    Marahute!
 (Cody looks at the eagle; he approaches her slowly; she hears him and wakes up; Marahute screeches and struggles to get free.)
 (reassuring) Calm down  calm down.  I'm not gonna hurt you.  (Cody strokes Marahute on the head) That's a girl. Stay still... it's o.k.
 (Cody gets out his knife; Marahute sees the glint of the knife and    begins to struggle and scream)
 No wait!  I'm here to help you... easy!... easy!
 (Cody cuts two ropes.  Cody cuts the last rope to free Marahute.)
 You're free!!
 (As Marahute spreads her wings to fly  she knocks Cody off the cliff.)
 Aaaiigh!
 (Cody falls; Marahute dives down to catch him; she catches him just before he hits the ground; they begin to fly around; the animals see Cody on Marahute and stand in awe; Marahute files over several rock formations; the fly up above the clouds; Cody looks at his reflection in Marahute's eye.)
 Higher!
 (They fly even higher above the clouds; Marahute throws Cody and catches him; Cody is now held in Marahute's talons.)
 Woah!
 (Cody mocks an eagle screech; he laughs as Marahute tickles him; they cruise above the clouds which eventually open up to show the ground; Marahute nose dives towards the ground and a stream; she holds Cody just high enough above the water so that he is water skiing; they approach a flock of birds; Marahute lets Cody go and he skims through the birds  scattering them; Marahute grabs Cody just before he falls in and then put Cody right in front of her  on her beak (pushing him from behind); they go over the egde of a waterfall; Marahute catches Cody again; this time he rides by standing on her back; they arrive at Marahute's nest)
 Wow!
 (Cody and Marahute look at each other; Cody falls over as he attempts to look at Marahute upside down. Marahute moves some grass and feathers to show Cody her eggs)
 You're a mom!
 (Cody puts his ear to the eggs)
 They're very warm.  Are they gonna hatch soon?
 (Marahute ruffles her neck feathers in an affectionate manner; she sits on the eggs and then looks out "over her domain".)
 Where's the daddy eagle?  (Marahute drops her head) Oh... my dad's gone too.
 (Cody give Marahute an affectionate stroke;  as they fix the covering on the eggs  the wind picks up and blows a feather in Cody's face; he looks at it  plays with it  and puts it back.  Marahute picks it up and gives it to Cody and he gives her a hug.)
 (Marahute and Cody are now on the ground; Marahute takes off and Cody runs around making flying noises)

(scene:  just inside the forest.  A wanted poster of McLeach is posted on a tree; A mouse is tied up with a bell attached to it that rings as it struggles; Cody hears the bell and goes over to the mouse.)

Cody:    Heh heh... hey little fella  what happened to you?

Baitmouse:    (panicking) Oh no! No  no  no  no!!  Get away  get away! It's a trap  it's a trap.  Be careful  NO!

Cody:    (as the mouse is speaking) Don't worry  I'll get you loose.  Woah! (Cody falls into the trap.  He looks up to see a blinking light. 

[Requirement] Based on this, continue the scene and develop the storyline. The continuation must be {word_count_type} {word_count} words long.

response:
**Scene: Deep in the forest – The Snare**

*The camera swings down to a tangled net of rope and thin steel wires glinting in the low light. Cody lands hard on a moss‑covered floor, his knife clattering from his hand. A faint, rhythmic ticking echoes from the rope – a tiny lantern hung from a branch above flickers, casting a pulsing amber glow.*

**CODY**  
*(groaning, rubbing his elbow)*  
Whoa… that’s… not what I was expecting.

*He scrambles to his feet, eyes darting around. The lantern’s light swings gently, as if being tugged by an unseen hand. The snare is clearly a trap set by poachers.*

**CODY** *(to himself)*  
Someone’s been here… but who?

*The lantern flickers faster, a rapid series of blinks like a Morse code. Cody leans closer.*

**CODES ON THE LANTERN**  
*(Morse flashes: “HELP”)*
  
**CODY**  
That can’t be… Poachers don’t ask for help.

*A rustle ahead. From the shadows steps a gaunt, wiry rat‑like creature—a **bandicoot** named **Jinx**, clutching a bundle of herbs.*

**JINX**  
*(whispering, eyes wide)*  
You… you’re the one they call “Cody the Brave.”  

**CODY**  
*(confused)*  
Who’s “they”? And why’d you…?

**JINX**  
The poachers… they set this trap for the **Golden Eagle**. The last one they caught was… *(voice falters)* …the egg‑watcher, Marahute’s mate. I… I was trying to get the herbs to heal the wound they gave him.  

**CODY**  
*(realising)*  
Marahute! She told me about the trap on the ridge. We’ve got to get that eagle out, and… you need to get out of there too.

**JINX**  
*(shaking his head)*  
It’s not just a net. See that blinking light? It’s a **radio beacon**. The poachers use it to track any movement near their camp. If it stays on, they’ll know we’re here.

*Jinx pulls a small leaf‑shaped device from his satchel. He flips a switch and the blinking stops.*

**JINX** *(continuing)*  
Now, we need to cut the ropes without pulling the whole thing down. The net’s attached to a **gravity‑trigger**—pull one too hard and the whole snare collapses.

**CODY**  
*(nods, gripping his knife)*  
Alright. Show me.

*Jinx points to a series of thin, almost invisible wires woven into the net. Cody kneels, examining them.*

**CODY** *(quietly)*  
These are the release lines. If we cut them in this order… *(he traces a pattern with his finger)* …it should free the trap without setting it off.

*Jinx watches, his ears twitching.*

**JINX**  
You sure you’ve done this before?

**CODY**  
First time on a poacher’s snare. But I’ve untied a lot of ropes.

*He takes a deep breath, then slices the first line with a swift, precise motion. The net shivers, but holds. He moves to the second, then the third. When the final line is cut, the net slumps gently to the forest floor, releasing a **large, glittering golden feather**.*

**JINX**  
*(eyes alight)*  
That’s a feather from Marahute’s… from the **queen’s plume**. It’s said to guide the true-hearted.

**CODY** *(holding the feather)*  
Let’s get you out of here, Jinx. And then we find Marahute.

*Jinx hops onto Cody’s shoulder, clinging to his jacket. Cody darts forward, leading the way through tangled underbrush. As they move, the camera pulls back, revealing a faint **glimmer** in the distance—an ancient, weather‑worn **treehouse** perched atop a massive eucalyptus, its windows glowing amber.*

**Scene: The Treehouse – “Faloo’s Nest”**

*The entrance is a hollowed-out trunk with a carved sign: “FALOO – GUARDIAN OF THE SKY.” Inside, a **large hollow log** serves as a table; lit by fireflies trapped in glass jars. **Faloo**, the bright‑plumed **kookaburra**, perches on a branch, eyes sharp.*

**FALOO** *(calling)*  
Cody! Jinx! You two look… tangled!

**CODY** *(breathing heavily)*  
Faloo! We’ve got a trap… a poacher’s net, a beacon, and… I think we’ve got a feather.

**FALOO** *(fluttering down, landing on Jinx’s head)*  
A feather from Marahute’s plume? That’s good news. The poachers have a **camp** not far from the ridge, near the **Crocodile Falls**. They’re hunting the golden eagles for their feathers. If they get their hands on Marahute’s mate… we’ve got trouble.

**JINX**  
*(shivering)*  
They’re already here. I heard their boots. They’re moving towards the ridge.

**FALOO**  
Then we must act fast. Cody, you’re the only one who can get up there without being seen. Marahute’s trust in you is strong. But we’ll need a distraction.

*Faloo flaps his wings, echoing the sound of drums. From behind a curtain of vines, a **band of kangaroos** emerge, each holding a **coconut‑shell drum**.*

**THE KANGAROOS** *(in unison)*  
We’re ready, Faloo! Let’s give ‘em a rhythm they won’t forget.

**CODY** *(smiling)*  
Alright, let’s do this.

**Scene: The River – “The Silent Approach”**

*The group moves out of the treehouse, slipping through the underbrush. Jinx clutches the golden feather, its iridescent sheen catching moonlight. Cody quietly tucks the feather into his pack. Fal

oo leads the way, his melodic call cutting through the night.*

*The camera follows them along a ribbon of water, the **River of Whispers**, which winds towards **Crocodile Falls**. The sound of water is punctuated by distant, low rumblings – the poachers’ heavy boots.*

**CODY** *(whispering to Faloo)*  
How far is the camp?

**FALOO** *(puffing his chest)*  
Two ridges up, then a hollow cliff‑side. The eagles’ nest is just past that. If we can create enough noise at the falls, the poachers will think a storm’s coming and retreat.

**JINX**  
I can rig a **boulder** to roll down the gorge. Little enough to scare, big enough to be heard.

**CODY** *(nodding)*  
Let’s split up. Jinx, you set the boulder. Faloo, you and the kangaroos start the drum beat near the falls. I’ll head to the ridge and–  

*The sound of **crackling twigs** echoes. A **shadow** looms into view – a **poacher**, massive, its face obscured by a **bandana**, holding a **rifle**.*

**POACHER LEADER**  
*(growling)*  
What have we here? A little mutt and his feathered friends. Looks like the eagles finally showed up for dinner.

**CODY** *(stepping forward, knife out)*  
Not today.

**POACHER LEADER**  
*(laughs)*  
You? One boy? You’ll make a nice addition to the mount.

*The other poachers, **three hulking figures**, emerge from behind boulders, the sound of **metal clanking** filling the night.*

**Scene: The Confrontation – “The Night of the Feather”**

*Jinx darts between the poachers, his small size making him hard to track. He tosses a handful of **glowing fireflies** at the poachers’ faces. The insects flicker and swarm, blinding them temporarily.*

**JINX** *(shouting)*  
Now, Cody! Get the feather!

*Cody lunges forward, his knife flashing as he slashes the rope of the **rifle’s scope**, causing the **aiming lens** to shatter. The rifle clatters to the ground, its muzzle whipping a spray of dust.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(snarling)*  
You’ll pay for that!

*Faloo and the kangaroos begin a **thunderous drumbeat** from the edge of the falls. The rhythm reverberates through the gorge, echoing off the cliffs. Simultaneously, Jinx kicks the **boulder** perched atop a small ledge. It teeters, then crashes down the gorge, striking the **water** with a deafening splash.*

*The roar of the falling boulder and the drumbeat merge into a **storm-like cacophony**. The poachers, startled, glance toward the source.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(panicked)*  
The river's swelling! We need to regroup!

*He gestures to his men, who scramble back toward the falls, weapons clattering.*

**CODY** *(to Faloo)*  
That’s our chance. Let’s move.

**Scene: The Ridge – “Ascending for Marahute”**

*The camera follows Cody as he scales the rugged ridge, his muscles straining. He brushes past **thick vines** and **sharp rocks**, the golden feather tucked in his pack. Above him, the sky darkens with **storm clouds**, the distant drumming of Faloo echoing like thunder.*

*At the crest, **Marahute’s nest** is perched on a jutting ledge, a **massive nest of twigs and feathers**. The golden eagle, **Marahute**, lies with a wing injured, her eyes weary but alert.*

**MARAHUTE** *(softly)*  
Cody… you came.

**CODY** *(kneeling, gently lowering his pack)*  
I’m here, Marahute. I brought something… a gift.

*He pulls out the **golden feather**. Marahute’s eyes widen, a flicker of recognition flashing.*

**MARAHUTE** *(whispering)*  
The queen’s plume… it’s a sign of trust. You have the heart of the sky.

*Cody presses the feather to her beak. Marahute’s **wing** tingles, the pain easing slightly.*

**MARAHUTE** *(with renewed strength)*  
The poachers… they took my mate, **Ari**. He’s trapped in the **cavern below the falls**. They plan to sell his feathers.

**CODY**  
We’ll get him. Faloo’s creating a diversion. Jinx is still out there. Let’s get you back in the sky.

*Marahute tests her wing, stretching cautiously. A gentle breeze lifts her, and with a powerful beat, she rises a few feet, then lands gracefully.*

**CODY** *(laughing)*  
Looks like you’re almost ready to fly again.

**Scene: The Final Assault – “Saving the Eagle”**

*Back at the falls, Faloo’s drumming intensifies, now synced with the howling wind. The poachers, disoriented, retreat towards their **cabin**—a grim, tar‑painted shack perched near the **cavern entrance**. The **cabin’s windows** glow with a faint **orange light**, indicating a **lamp** inside.*

*Jinx, still darting through shadows, finds the **cabin door** slightly ajar. He slips inside, his eyes adjusting to the dim interior. Inside, a **map** of the forest hangs on the wall, marked with **X’s** on the **eagle nest**, **canyon**, and **waterfall**.*

**JINX** *(to himself)*  
There… the **cavern**. They’ve set a **trapdoor** here. If I can open it from the inside…

*Jinx darts toward a rusted **metal lever**. He pulls it, and the **trapdoor** groans open, revealing a **dark tunnel**.*

**JINX** *(calling out)*  
Cody! I’ve got a way in!

*Cody, now at the ridge edge, looks down at the cavern mouth. He nods.*

**CODY** *(shouting)*  
Jinx! Bring them out!

*Jinx rushes back out, clutching a **rope** tied to a **pallet** of **cannons of meat**—the poachers’ bait. He slides the rope down the cavern’s edge, securing it firmly. Faloo, perched on a rock above, lets out a sharp, rallying call, urging the **canyon bats** to swarm the poachers.*

*The **bats** swoop in, a black cloud descending on the cabin. The poachers, hearing the frantic screeches, scramble to the entrance of the cavern, their rifles aimed at the darkness.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(frustrated)*  
Where are they? Get the trap open!

*Jinx, with a swift kick, dislodges the **batteries** of the trapdoor, causing the **cave walls** to tremble. Dust falls, and a **cave‑spider web**—thin but strong—covers the entrance. The poachers, startled, are **snared by their own nets**.*

**CODY** *(climbing down the rope)*  
Hang on, Ari! I’m coming!

*Inside the cavern, the dim light reveals **Ari**, a majestic golden eagle with a **sore wing**, tied to a **rocky ledge**. His eyes widen as Cody reaches him.*

**ARI** *(weak but hopeful)*  
Cody… you made it.

**CODY** *(cutting the ropes with his knife)*  
We’re getting you out. Hold on.

*As Cody frees Ari, the **cave** begins to **rumble**—the poachers’ trap is collapsing. Stone dust falls, but Jinx, who has already thrown a **large wooden log** to block the entrance, holds it steady.*

**JINX** *(shouting)*  
Run, Ari! The exit’s clear!

*Ari spreads his wings, the same golden sheen as Marahute, and takes off, soaring through the narrow tunnel. Cody follows, clutching the rope, his heart pounding.*

**Scene: The Escape – “Storm Over the Falls”**

*The group bursts out of the cavern just as the **trapdoor** slams shut behind them, sealing the poachers inside. The **cabin** erupts in a **burst of flames**, lit by an accidental spark from Faloo’s drumming sticks striking a **metal pipe**.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(screaming, trapped)*  
No! Get out of my way!

*The fire spreads rapidly, casting a **golden-orange glow** over the cliffs. The **Crocodile Falls** thunder louder, water cascading in a **roaring cascade**.*

*Cody, Ari, Marahute, and Faloo stand at the edge of the falls, the poachers’ cabin now a **smoldering ruin**.*

**MARAHUTE** *(soaring higher)*  
You did it, Cody. You saved us all.

**CODY** *(grinning, wind whipping his hair)*  
We didn’t do it alone. This forest—our friends—made it happen.

**FALOO** *(proudly puffing his chest)*  
It’s a new day for the outback. Let’s make sure it stays that way.

**Scene: The Celebration – “The Great Sky Festival”**

*The sun rises over the **Australian outback**, painting the sky with shades of pink and amber. A **gathering** of forest creatures forms a massive circle around a **clearing** near the base of the falls. **Kookaburras**, **kangaroos**, **wombats**, **bandicoots**, **kangaroo rats**, and even a **family of echidnas** are present.*

*In the center, a **large wooden platform** has been built. Marahute and Ari perch atop it, their wings spread wide, their golden feathers catching the sunrise. Cody stands beside them, holding the **golden queen’s feather** aloft.*

**CODY** *(addressing the crowd)*  
Friends, today we rescued Marahute’s companion and showed that together we can protect our home. Let’s promise to keep this land safe from those who would harm it.

*The crowd erupts in joyful **chirps, hisses, and thumps**. The **kangaroos** begin a rhythmic **stomp dance**, their feet creating a low, resonant beat that reverberates through the earth.*

*Faloo flies above, his **call** echoing like a trumpet, signaling the start of the **Great Sky Festival**. The **eagles** swoop down, dropping **cascading ribbons of sunlight** onto the gathering.*

*Jinx, perched on a low branch, pulls out a **tiny wooden flute** and begins a melodic tune. The melody weaves through the sounds of drums and stomps, creating a harmonious symphony.*

**MARAHUTE** *(softly, to Cody)*  
You have the heart of a sky‑rider. Will you stay and watch over us?

**CODY** *(looking at his mother’s house in the distance, then back at the forest)*  
My home will always be there, but my heart belongs wherever the wind takes me. I’ll be here, as long as the sky is blue.

*The **golden feather** in Cody’s hands begins to **glow softly**, casting a warm light on the faces of those around.*

**Scene: The Return Home – “A New Dawn”**

*Later, as the festival winds down, the forest animals begin to disperse. Cody walks toward the **path that leads back to his house**, the **hummingbird** he saw at the start of his adventure fluttering beside him.*

*He pauses at a **large, ancient gum tree**, where a **carved wooden sign** reads: “Cody’s Trail – Protector of the Outback.” He smiles, placing the **golden feather** into a **hollow** in the tree, where it will shine for generations.*

**CODY** *(whispering to the wind)*  
Until next time, friends.

*He continues down the trail, the camera pulling back to reveal the **vast expanse** of the outback – the red earth stretching toward the horizon, the **Ayres Rock** standing majestically in the distance, and the **sun** climbing higher.*

**Scene: Inside Cody’s House – “Morning Light”**

*The door creaks open. **Mom** is at the kitchen table, a **plate of fresh fruit** and a **warm slice of toast** waiting. She looks up, eyes wide with relief and pride.*

**MOM** *(embracing Cody)*  
You’re back! I was so worried.

**CODY** *(still wind‑blown, smiling)*  
I’m okay, Mom. I— I met some amazing friends out there.

*He places his **backpack** down, revealing the **old map** of the outback he had once drawn as a child, now marked with **new routes** and **tiny feather symbols**.*

**MOM** *(laughing)*  
Looks like you’ve been on quite the adventure.

*Outside, the **birdsong** rises, and a **soft gust** carries a faint echo of Faloo’s call.*

**CODY** *(to his mother, a hint of mischief in his eyes)*  
You know, Mom, I think I’m going to need a bigger sandwich for tomorrow.

*The camera pans up to the **sky**, where a **silhouette of an eagle** swoops across the sunrise, its wings catching the first golden rays.*

**FADE OUT.**

**THE END – BUT THE SKY IS ALWAYS OPEN.**
output:
**Scene: Deep in the forest – The Snare**

*The camera swings down to a tangled net of rope and thin steel wires glinting in the low light. Cody lands hard on a moss‑covered floor, his knife clattering from his hand. A faint, rhythmic ticking echoes from the rope – a tiny lantern hung from a branch above flickers, casting a pulsing amber glow.*

**CODY**  
*(groaning, rubbing his elbow)*  
Whoa… that’s… not what I was expecting.

*He scrambles to his feet, eyes darting around. The lantern’s light swings gently, as if being tugged by an unseen hand. The snare is clearly a trap set by poachers.*

**CODY** *(to himself)*  
Someone’s been here… but who?

*The lantern flickers faster, a rapid series of blinks like a Morse code. Cody leans closer.*

**CODES ON THE LANTERN**  
*(Morse flashes: “HELP”)*
  
**CODY**  
That can’t be… Poachers don’t ask for help.

*A rustle ahead. From the shadows steps a gaunt, wiry rat‑like creature—a **bandicoot** named **Jinx**, clutching a bundle of herbs.*

**JINX**  
*(whispering, eyes wide)*  
You… you’re the one they call “Cody the Brave.”  

**CODY**  
*(confused)*  
Who’s “they”? And why’d you…?

**JINX**  
The poachers… they set this trap for the **Golden Eagle**. The last one they caught was… *(voice falters)* …the egg‑watcher, Marahute’s mate. I… I was trying to get the herbs to heal the wound they gave him.  

**CODY**  
*(realising)*  
Marahute! She told me about the trap on the ridge. We’ve got to get that eagle out, and… you need to get out of there too.

**JINX**  
*(shaking his head)*  
It’s not just a net. See that blinking light? It’s a **radio beacon**. The poachers use it to track any movement near their camp. If it stays on, they’ll know we’re here.

*Jinx pulls a small leaf‑shaped device from his satchel. He flips a switch and the blinking stops.*

**JINX** *(continuing)*  
Now, we need to cut the ropes without pulling the whole thing down. The net’s attached to a **gravity‑trigger**—pull one too hard and the whole snare collapses.

**CODY**  
*(nods, gripping his knife)*  
Alright. Show me.

*Jinx points to a series of thin, almost invisible wires woven into the net. Cody kneels, examining them.*

**CODY** *(quietly)*  
These are the release lines. If we cut them in this order… *(he traces a pattern with his finger)* …it should free the trap without setting it off.

*Jinx watches, his ears twitching.*

**JINX**  
You sure you’ve done this before?

**CODY**  
First time on a poacher’s snare. But I’ve untied a lot of ropes.

*He takes a deep breath, then slices the first line with a swift, precise motion. The net shivers, but holds. He moves to the second, then the third. When the final line is cut, the net slumps gently to the forest floor, releasing a **large, glittering golden feather**.*

**JINX**  
*(eyes alight)*  
That’s a feather from Marahute’s… from the **queen’s plume**. It’s said to guide the true-hearted.

**CODY** *(holding the feather)*  
Let’s get you out of here, Jinx. And then we find Marahute.

*Jinx hops onto Cody’s shoulder, clinging to his jacket. Cody darts forward, leading the way through tangled underbrush. As they move, the camera pulls back, revealing a faint **glimmer** in the distance—an ancient, weather‑worn **treehouse** perched atop a massive eucalyptus, its windows glowing amber.*

**Scene: The Treehouse – “Faloo’s Nest”**

*The entrance is a hollowed-out trunk with a carved sign: “FALOO – GUARDIAN OF THE SKY.” Inside, a **large hollow log** serves as a table; lit by fireflies trapped in glass jars. **Faloo**, the bright‑plumed **kookaburra**, perches on a branch, eyes sharp.*

**FALOO** *(calling)*  
Cody! Jinx! You two look… tangled!

**CODY** *(breathing heavily)*  
Faloo! We’ve got a trap… a poacher’s net, a beacon, and… I think we’ve got a feather.

**FALOO** *(fluttering down, landing on Jinx’s head)*  
A feather from Marahute’s plume? That’s good news. The poachers have a **camp** not far from the ridge, near the **Crocodile Falls**. They’re hunting the golden eagles for their feathers. If they get their hands on Marahute’s mate… we’ve got trouble.

**JINX**  
*(shivering)*  
They’re already here. I heard their boots. They’re moving towards the ridge.

**FALOO**  
Then we must act fast. Cody, you’re the only one who can get up there without being seen. Marahute’s trust in you is strong. But we’ll need a distraction.

*Faloo flaps his wings, echoing the sound of drums. From behind a curtain of vines, a **band of kangaroos** emerge, each holding a **coconut‑shell drum**.*

**THE KANGAROOS** *(in unison)*  
We’re ready, Faloo! Let’s give ‘em a rhythm they won’t forget.

**CODY** *(smiling)*  
Alright, let’s do this.

**Scene: The River – “The Silent Approach”**

*The group moves out of the treehouse, slipping through the underbrush. Jinx clutches the golden feather, its iridescent sheen catching moonlight. Cody quietly tucks the feather into his pack. Fal

oo leads the way, his melodic call cutting through the night.*

*The camera follows them along a ribbon of water, the **River of Whispers**, which winds towards **Crocodile Falls**. The sound of water is punctuated by distant, low rumblings – the poachers’ heavy boots.*

**CODY** *(whispering to Faloo)*  
How far is the camp?

**FALOO** *(puffing his chest)*  
Two ridges up, then a hollow cliff‑side. The eagles’ nest is just past that. If we can create enough noise at the falls, the poachers will think a storm’s coming and retreat.

**JINX**  
I can rig a **boulder** to roll down the gorge. Little enough to scare, big enough to be heard.

**CODY** *(nodding)*  
Let’s split up. Jinx, you set the boulder. Faloo, you and the kangaroos start the drum beat near the falls. I’ll head to the ridge and–  

*The sound of **crackling twigs** echoes. A **shadow** looms into view – a **poacher**, massive, its face obscured by a **bandana**, holding a **rifle**.*

**POACHER LEADER**  
*(growling)*  
What have we here? A little mutt and his feathered friends. Looks like the eagles finally showed up for dinner.

**CODY** *(stepping forward, knife out)*  
Not today.

**POACHER LEADER**  
*(laughs)*  
You? One boy? You’ll make a nice addition to the mount.

*The other poachers, **three hulking figures**, emerge from behind boulders, the sound of **metal clanking** filling the night.*

**Scene: The Confrontation – “The Night of the Feather”**

*Jinx darts between the poachers, his small size making him hard to track. He tosses a handful of **glowing fireflies** at the poachers’ faces. The insects flicker and swarm, blinding them temporarily.*

**JINX** *(shouting)*  
Now, Cody! Get the feather!

*Cody lunges forward, his knife flashing as he slashes the rope of the **rifle’s scope**, causing the **aiming lens** to shatter. The rifle clatters to the ground, its muzzle whipping a spray of dust.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(snarling)*  
You’ll pay for that!

*Faloo and the kangaroos begin a **thunderous drumbeat** from the edge of the falls. The rhythm reverberates through the gorge, echoing off the cliffs. Simultaneously, Jinx kicks the **boulder** perched atop a small ledge. It teeters, then crashes down the gorge, striking the **water** with a deafening splash.*

*The roar of the falling boulder and the drumbeat merge into a **storm-like cacophony**. The poachers, startled, glance toward the source.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(panicked)*  
The river's swelling! We need to regroup!

*He gestures to his men, who scramble back toward the falls, weapons clattering.*

**CODY** *(to Faloo)*  
That’s our chance. Let’s move.

**Scene: The Ridge – “Ascending for Marahute”**

*The camera follows Cody as he scales the rugged ridge, his muscles straining. He brushes past **thick vines** and **sharp rocks**, the golden feather tucked in his pack. Above him, the sky darkens with **storm clouds**, the distant drumming of Faloo echoing like thunder.*

*At the crest, **Marahute’s nest** is perched on a jutting ledge, a **massive nest of twigs and feathers**. The golden eagle, **Marahute**, lies with a wing injured, her eyes weary but alert.*

**MARAHUTE** *(softly)*  
Cody… you came.

**CODY** *(kneeling, gently lowering his pack)*  
I’m here, Marahute. I brought something… a gift.

*He pulls out the **golden feather**. Marahute’s eyes widen, a flicker of recognition flashing.*

**MARAHUTE** *(whispering)*  
The queen’s plume… it’s a sign of trust. You have the heart of the sky.

*Cody presses the feather to her beak. Marahute’s **wing** tingles, the pain easing slightly.*

**MARAHUTE** *(with renewed strength)*  
The poachers… they took my mate, **Ari**. He’s trapped in the **cavern below the falls**. They plan to sell his feathers.

**CODY**  
We’ll get him. Faloo’s creating a diversion. Jinx is still out there. Let’s get you back in the sky.

*Marahute tests her wing, stretching cautiously. A gentle breeze lifts her, and with a powerful beat, she rises a few feet, then lands gracefully.*

**CODY** *(laughing)*  
Looks like you’re almost ready to fly again.

**Scene: The Final Assault – “Saving the Eagle”**

*Back at the falls, Faloo’s drumming intensifies, now synced with the howling wind. The poachers, disoriented, retreat towards their **cabin**—a grim, tar‑painted shack perched near the **cavern entrance**. The **cabin’s windows** glow with a faint **orange light**, indicating a **lamp** inside.*

*Jinx, still darting through shadows, finds the **cabin door** slightly ajar. He slips inside, his eyes adjusting to the dim interior. Inside, a **map** of the forest hangs on the wall, marked with **X’s** on the **eagle nest**, **canyon**, and **waterfall**.*

**JINX** *(to himself)*  
There… the **cavern**. They’ve set a **trapdoor** here. If I can open it from the inside…

*Jinx darts toward a rusted **metal lever**. He pulls it, and the **trapdoor** groans open, revealing a **dark tunnel**.*

**JINX** *(calling out)*  
Cody! I’ve got a way in!

*Cody, now at the ridge edge, looks down at the cavern mouth. He nods.*

**CODY** *(shouting)*  
Jinx! Bring them out!

*Jinx rushes back out, clutching a **rope** tied to a **pallet** of **cannons of meat**—the poachers’ bait. He slides the rope down the cavern’s edge, securing it firmly. Faloo, perched on a rock above, lets out a sharp, rallying call, urging the **canyon bats** to swarm the poachers.*

*The **bats** swoop in, a black cloud descending on the cabin. The poachers, hearing the frantic screeches, scramble to the entrance of the cavern, their rifles aimed at the darkness.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(frustrated)*  
Where are they? Get the trap open!

*Jinx, with a swift kick, dislodges the **batteries** of the trapdoor, causing the **cave walls** to tremble. Dust falls, and a **cave‑spider web**—thin but strong—covers the entrance. The poachers, startled, are **snared by their own nets**.*

**CODY** *(climbing down the rope)*  
Hang on, Ari! I’m coming!

*Inside the cavern, the dim light reveals **Ari**, a majestic golden eagle with a **sore wing**, tied to a **rocky ledge**. His eyes widen as Cody reaches him.*

**ARI** *(weak but hopeful)*  
Cody… you made it.

**CODY** *(cutting the ropes with his knife)*  
We’re getting you out. Hold on.

*As Cody frees Ari, the **cave** begins to **rumble**—the poachers’ trap is collapsing. Stone dust falls, but Jinx, who has already thrown a **large wooden log** to block the entrance, holds it steady.*

**JINX** *(shouting)*  
Run, Ari! The exit’s clear!

*Ari spreads his wings, the same golden sheen as Marahute, and takes off, soaring through the narrow tunnel. Cody follows, clutching the rope, his heart pounding.*

**Scene: The Escape – “Storm Over the Falls”**

*The group bursts out of the cavern just as the **trapdoor** slams shut behind them, sealing the poachers inside. The **cabin** erupts in a **burst of flames**, lit by an accidental spark from Faloo’s drumming sticks striking a **metal pipe**.*

**POACHER LEADER** *(screaming, trapped)*  
No! Get out of my way!

*The fire spreads rapidly, casting a **golden-orange glow** over the cliffs. The **Crocodile Falls** thunder louder, water cascading in a **roaring cascade**.*

*Cody, Ari, Marahute, and Faloo stand at the edge of the falls, the poachers’ cabin now a **smoldering ruin**.*

**MARAHUTE** *(soaring higher)*  
You did it, Cody. You saved us all.

**CODY** *(grinning, wind whipping his hair)*  
We didn’t do it alone. This forest—our friends—made it happen.

**FALOO** *(proudly puffing his chest)*  
It’s a new day for the outback. Let’s make sure it stays that way.

**Scene: The Celebration – “The Great Sky Festival”**

*The sun rises over the **Australian outback**, painting the sky with shades of pink and amber. A **gathering** of forest creatures forms a massive circle around a **clearing** near the base of the falls. **Kookaburras**, **kangaroos**, **wombats**, **bandicoots**, **kangaroo rats**, and even a **family of echidnas** are present.*

*In the center, a **large wooden platform** has been built. Marahute and Ari perch atop it, their wings spread wide, their golden feathers catching the sunrise. Cody stands beside them, holding the **golden queen’s feather** aloft.*

**CODY** *(addressing the crowd)*  
Friends, today we rescued Marahute’s companion and showed that together we can protect our home. Let’s promise to keep this land safe from those who would harm it.

*The crowd erupts in joyful **chirps, hisses, and thumps**. The **kangaroos** begin a rhythmic **stomp dance**, their feet creating a low, resonant beat that reverberates through the earth.*

*Faloo flies above, his **call** echoing like a trumpet, signaling the start of the **Great Sky Festival**. The **eagles** swoop down, dropping **cascading ribbons of sunlight** onto the gathering.*

*Jinx, perched on a low branch, pulls out a **tiny wooden flute** and begins a melodic tune. The melody weaves through the sounds of drums and stomps, creating a harmonious symphony.*

**MARAHUTE** *(softly, to Cody)*  
You have the heart of a sky‑rider. Will you stay and watch over us?

**CODY** *(looking at his mother’s house in the distance, then back at the forest)*  
My home will always be there, but my heart belongs wherever the wind takes me. I’ll be here, as long as the sky is blue.

*The **golden feather** in Cody’s hands begins to **glow softly**, casting a warm light on the faces of those around.*

**Scene: The Return Home – “A New Dawn”**

*Later, as the festival winds down, the forest animals begin to disperse. Cody walks toward the **path that leads back to his house**, the **hummingbird** he saw at the start of his adventure fluttering beside him.*

*He pauses at a **large, ancient gum tree**, where a **carved wooden sign** reads: “Cody’s Trail – Protector of the Outback.” He smiles, placing the **golden feather** into a **hollow** in the tree, where it will shine for generations.*

**CODY** *(whispering to the wind)*  
Until next time, friends.

*He continues down the trail, the camera pulling back to reveal the **vast expanse** of the outback – the red earth stretching toward the horizon, the **Ayres Rock** standing majestically in the distance, and the **sun** climbing higher.*

**Scene: Inside Cody’s House – “Morning Light”**

*The door creaks open. **Mom** is at the kitchen table, a **plate of fresh fruit** and a **warm slice of toast** waiting. She looks up, eyes wide with relief and pride.*

**MOM** *(embracing Cody)*  
You’re back! I was so worried.

**CODY** *(still wind‑blown, smiling)*  
I’m okay, Mom. I— I met some amazing friends out there.

*He places his **backpack** down, revealing the **old map** of the outback he had once drawn as a child, now marked with **new routes** and **tiny feather symbols**.*

**MOM** *(laughing)*  
Looks like you’ve been on quite the adventure.

*Outside, the **birdsong** rises, and a **soft gust** carries a faint echo of Faloo’s call.*

**CODY** *(to his mother, a hint of mischief in his eyes)*  
You know, Mom, I think I’m going to need a bigger sandwich for tomorrow.

*The camera pans up to the **sky**, where a **silhouette of an eagle** swoops across the sunrise, its wings catching the first golden rays.*

**FADE OUT.**

**THE END – BUT THE SKY IS ALWAYS OPEN.**

response:
**Taraji P. Henson’s Son Allegedly Racially Profiled by USC Police: A Growing Controversy on Campus Safety**

*Los Angeles, April 29, 2026* — Actress Taraji P. Henson has publicly accused university police officers of racially profiling her 19‑year‑old son, Marcel, during a routine stop on the University of Southern California (USC) campus. The claim has sparked a wave of criticism directed at USC’s Public Safety Department and has prompted calls for an independent review of the university’s policing practices.

### The Incident

According to Henson, Marcel was stopped on a Thursday evening near the USC student union while walking back from a study group. “They approached him, asked for his ID, and kept asking the same questions over and over,” Henson recounted in a statement released through her publicist. “I know my son. He was just trying to get home. The whole interaction felt like an intimidation tactic based solely on his skin color.”

Henson’s family says Marcel was not violating any campus policies, nor was he engaged in any suspicious activity. “He was simply walking across campus with a backpack full of textbooks,” Henson added. “There was no reason for officers to detain him, yet they chose to do so, and the language they used was harsh and demeaning.”

### USC Public Safety Responds

USC’s Public Safety Director, Captain Luis Ramirez, issued a brief response that declined to comment on the specifics of the incident, citing ongoing investigations and privacy concerns. However, he did acknowledge that racial profiling is an issue that the department has been grappling with for years.

“Public safety is a core responsibility of any university, and we must ensure that all members of our community are treated with dignity and respect,” Ramirez said in an internal memo leaked to the campus newspaper *The Daily Trojan*. “We have implemented bias‑training programs and are reviewing our stop‑and‑question protocols to eliminate any form of discrimination.”

Notably, Ramirez disclosed that he himself has experienced what he believes to be racial profiling while on duty. In a candid interview with *Los Angeles Times* earlier this month, he recounted an episode in which an off‑campus officer stopped his patrol car for a minor traffic violation and subjected him to a prolonged, hostile interrogation solely because of his Hispanic heritage.

“It’s not just about the community; it’s about us, the officers, who are sometimes caught in the crossfire of stereotypes,” Ramirez said. “I’ve been on both sides of this equation, and it underscores the need for systemic change.”

### A Call for Independent Investigation

John Thomas, a prominent civil‑rights attorney and longtime advocate for police reform, announced he will spearhead an independent investigation into the alleged profiling incident. Thomas, who has previously represented high‑profile cases involving police misconduct in Los Angeles and New York, said his involvement was prompted by a request from the Henson family.

“While the university can conduct its own internal review, a truly impartial investigation requires a third party with no vested interest in USC’s reputation or finances,” Thomas told reporters at a press conference outside the USC campus. “Our team will interview all parties, examine body‑camera footage, review policy manuals, and assess whether the actions taken against Marcel were consistent with procedural standards or indicative of bias.”

Thomas also pledged to release a comprehensive report within 90 days, complete with recommendations for policy reforms, mandatory bias training, and potential disciplinary actions if misconduct is confirmed.

### The Broader Context: Policing on College Campuses

The Henson incident arrives at a time when college campuses nationwide are scrutinizing the role of campus police in an increasingly polarized social climate. A 2024 study by the Pew Research Center found that 61 percent of college students of color felt “uneasy” or “very uneasy” about interactions with campus security officers. Critics argue that university police forces, often modeled after municipal departments, inherit the same systemic biases and lack the oversight mechanisms present in public law enforcement.

USC’s Public Safety Department, which employs over 300 sworn officers and 150 civilian staff, has faced several controversies in recent years, ranging from allegations of excessive force during a protest in 2022 to complaints of discriminatory ticketing practices in 2023. In response, the university established a Campus Safety Advisory Council in 2024, comprised of faculty, students, and community leaders, tasked with reviewing policing policies and recommending reforms.

Yet, many activists contend that the council’s recommendations have been slow to materialize. “We’ve been hearing the same promises for years,” said Maya Patel, a senior studying sociology and a member of the student coalition Black Students United. “Now we have a high‑profile case that underscores why those promises must translate into concrete action.”

### Reactions from the Entertainment Community

The allegation has ignited an outpouring of support from fellow actors and industry professionals. Oscar‑winning actress Regina King, a longtime friend of Henson, posted a photo of her and Henson on Instagram with the caption, “Standing with my sister. No one should be harassed for the color of their skin, especially not a child.”

The Screen Actors Guild‑American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG‑AFTRA) issued a statement expressing solidarity with the Henson family and urging “all law‑enforcement entities, including those on college campuses, to adopt zero‑tolerance policies for racial profiling.”

### Potential Legal Outcomes

If Thomas’ investigation confirms that the USC officers violated anti‑discrimination statutes, the Henson family could pursue both civil and criminal remedies. California’s Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) prohibits discrimination based on race, color, and national origin in public accommodations, which courts have increasingly applied to law‑enforcement conduct.

Moreover, federal statutes such as 42 U.S.C. § 1983, which allows individuals to sue state actors for constitutional violations, may also be invoked, should evidence point to a breach of the Fourteenth Amendment's Equal Protection Clause.

Legal experts caution, however, that proving “racial profiling” can be challenging. “The key is establishing that race was the primary motivation for the stop, not just a factor among many,” said Professor Elena Ramirez, a civil‑rights scholar at UCLA School of Law. “Video evidence, officer testimonies, and patterns of similar incidents will be crucial.”

### What Comes Next

USC administration has pledged full cooperation with any external inquiry. President Dr. Carol L. Folt sent an email to the university community stating, “The safety and dignity of our students, staff, and visitors is non‑negotiable. We will take all necessary steps to ensure accountability and restore confidence in our public safety operations.”

In the meantime, the Henson family has requested a temporary suspension of any disciplinary actions against Marcel while the investigation proceeds. Marcel, who is a sophomore majoring in computer science, has not spoken publicly about the incident but has been seen on campus today walking with friends, according to eyewitnesses.

The unfolding situation underscores a larger national dialogue about policing, race, and institutional responsibility. As the investigation moves forward, many will be watching closely to see whether USC can turn rhetoric into reform, and whether the Henson family’s call for justice will set a precedent for how universities address allegations of racial profiling on their grounds.

*Reporting by* **Karen Liu** *and* **James O’Connor** *for* **The Los Angeles Times**. *Edited by* **Maria Gomez**.

### A Call for Independent Investigation

John Thomas, a prominent civil‑rights attorney and longtime advocate for police reform, announced he will spearhead an independent investigation into the alleged profiling incident. Thomas, who has previously represented high‑profile cases involving police misconduct in Los Angeles and New York, said his involvement was prompted by a request from the Henson family.

“While the university can conduct its own internal review, a truly impartial investigation requires a third party with no vested interest in USC’s reputation or finances,” Thomas told reporters at a press conference outside the USC campus. “Our team will interview all parties, examine body‑camera footage, review policy manuals, and assess whether the actions taken against Marcel were consistent with procedural standards or indicative of bias.”

Thomas also pledged to release a comprehensive report within 90 days, complete with recommendations for policy reforms, mandatory bias training, and potential disciplinary actions if misconduct is confirmed.

### The Broader Context: Policing on College Campuses

The Henson incident arrives at a time when college campuses nationwide are scrutinizing the role of campus police in an increasingly polarized social climate. A 2024 study by the Pew Research Center found that 61 percent of college students of color felt “uneasy” or “very uneasy” about interactions with campus security officers. Critics argue that university police forces, often modeled after municipal departments, inherit the same systemic biases and lack the oversight mechanisms present in public law enforcement.

USC’s Public Safety Department, which employs over 300 sworn officers and 150 civilian staff, has faced several controversies in recent years, ranging from allegations of excessive force during a protest in 2022 to complaints of discriminatory ticketing practices in 2023. In response, the university established a Campus Safety Advisory Council in 2024, comprised of faculty, students, and community leaders, tasked with reviewing policing policies and recommending reforms.

Yet, many activists contend that the council’s recommendations have been slow to materialize. “We’ve been hearing the same promises for years,” said Maya Patel, a senior studying sociology and a member of the student coalition Black Students United. “Now we have a high‑profile case that underscores why those promises must translate into concrete action.”

### Reactions from the Entertainment Community

The allegation has ignited an outpouring of support from fellow actors and industry professionals. Oscar‑winning actress Regina King, a longtime friend of Henson, posted a photo of her and Henson on Instagram with the caption, “Standing with my sister. No one should be harassed for the color of their skin, especially not a child.”

The Screen Actors Guild‑American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG‑AFTRA) issued a statement expressing solidarity with the Henson family and urging “all law‑enforcement entities, including those on college campuses, to adopt zero‑tolerance policies for racial profiling.”

### Potential Legal Outcomes

If Thomas’ investigation confirms that the USC officers violated anti‑discrimination statutes, the Henson family could pursue both civil and criminal remedies. California’s Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) prohibits discrimination based on race, color, and national origin in public accommodations, which courts have increasingly applied to law‑enforcement conduct.

Moreover, federal statutes such as 42 U.S.C. § 1983, which allows individuals to sue state actors for constitutional violations, may also be invoked, should evidence point to a breach of the Fourteenth Amendment's Equal Protection Clause.

Legal experts caution, however, that proving “racial profiling” can be challenging. “The key is establishing that race was the primary motivation for the stop, not just a factor among many,” said Professor Elena Ramirez, a civil‑rights scholar at UCLA School of Law. “Video evidence, officer testimonies, and patterns of similar incidents will be crucial.”

### What Comes Next

USC administration has pledged full cooperation with any external inquiry. President Dr. Carol L. Folt sent an email to the university community stating, “The safety and dignity of our students, staff, and visitors is non‑negotiable. We will take all necessary steps to ensure accountability and restore confidence in our public safety operations.”

In the meantime, the Henson family has requested a temporary suspension of any disciplinary actions against Marcel while the investigation proceeds. Marcel, who is a sophomore majoring in computer science, has not spoken publicly about the incident but has been seen on campus today walking with friends, according to eyewitnesses.

The unfolding situation underscores a larger national dialogue about policing, race, and institutional responsibility. As the investigation moves forward, many will be watching closely to see whether USC can turn rhetoric into reform, and whether the Henson family’s call for justice will set a precedent for how universities address allegations of racial profiling on their grounds.
